top_signals.title: Is Time on China’s Side? Beijing’s Taiwan Calculus and the Balance of Power
top_signals.summary:
When is the risk of war the highest? And what should the United States be doing about it? One of the most important but underappreciated questions in international politics is how states think about the future balance of power. Countries that believe their position is improving often choose patience. Those who fear their position is deteriorating may feel pressure to act before their advantages disappear. In this episode, Ryan is joined by Dean Cheng, Mira Rapp-Hooper, and Amanda Hsiao to explore how Chinese leaders may be thinking about time, power, and Taiwan. This episode is sponsored by Kibu, which ensures you always know

The post Is Time on China’s Side? Beijing’s Taiwan Calculus and the Balance of Power appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Logistics Firm GLP Aims for $2 Billion of Asset Sales This Year
top_signals.summary: GLP Pte. told some bondholders during meetings in recent days that it aims to sell around $2 billion of assets this year, according to people familiar with the matter, as the logistics operator prepares for an initial public offering.

top_signals.title: Prosecutors seek 27-year sentence for suspect in murder of Hokkaido girl
top_signals.summary: Public prosecutors have alleged that Riko Uchida and others "applied some physical force" to the high school girl, who was embroiled in a dispute over social media photos.

top_signals.title: Australia's Far-Right One Nation Tops Poll
top_signals.summary:
Australia's populist One Nation party has surged past Labor in a nationwide opinion poll for the first time, as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s popularity hits fresh lows. Redbridge Director of Strategy and Analytics Kos Samaras discusses Australia's political landscape and voter dissatisfaction on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade."
 (Source: Bloomberg)
